reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku (non-Tokyo). Tool returned generic municipal admin pages from Akita (lifestyle/experience/food culture) with no cherry blossom content. None of the 30 results are sakura-specific or scenic spots.
missing_data: Named sakura spots in Tohoku: Hirosaki Park (Aomori), Kakunodate (Akita), Kitakami Tenshochi (Iwate), Miharu Takizakura (Fukushima).
improvement_hint: Add keyword-filtered spot retrieval or a seasonal/theme index so queries for 'sakura' return relevant named viewpoints rather than generic municipal tourism category pages.

reasoning: Query asks for sake breweries in Niigata. Tool used get_local_specialty which returns GI/METI food designations. Returns edamame, carrots, lotus root — no sake breweries at all. Completely wrong semantic category even though data is factually accurate.
missing_data: Niigata sake GI (越後杜氏 craft designation), major breweries: Hakkaisan, Kubota, Koshi no Kanbai. Sake is the defining specialty of Niigata.
improvement_hint: Add sake/beverage as a specialty category or alias 'sake brewery' queries to the craft/beverage GI dataset; Niigata has multiple sake GI designations that should surface here.

reasoning: Only 2 results for Hakuba, both administrative/press release pages (UN Tourism award, generic tourism admin). Zero ski resort or onsen content. Total_before_limit=2 confirms data sparsity for this municipality.
missing_data: Hakuba ski areas (Happo-one, Goryu, Tsugaike), hot spring facilities (Mimizuku-no-yu, Happo-no-yu), combined ski+onsen resort info.
improvement_hint: Expand municipal scrape coverage for Hakuba-mura; the current 2-result corpus is insufficient. Also consider adding Wikidata/OSM cross-reference for ski areas to supplement thin municipal data.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage in Kansai. Tool returned Kyoto spots but results are an art festival and a silk weaving heritage page from Kyotango — no autumn foliage spots. Retrieval fetches Kyoto city admin data but misses the actual intent entirely.
missing_data: Kiyomizudera, Tofukuji, Arashiyama (Kyoto); Nara Yoshino; Osaka Minoo. These are Kansai's canonical koyo destinations.
improvement_hint: Implement keyword/theme filtering in get_spots so seasonal queries (koyo, sakura) return only tourist-attraction-type records, not administrative news pages.

reasoning: Nara query for Kansai sakura. One result (Yamato-Takada Senbonzakura, 2.5km canal cherry blossom boulevard, near Kintetsu) is genuinely useful. However most returned spots are admin/event pages burying this single relevant entry. Yoshino (most famous Kansai sakura) absent.
missing_data: Yoshino (Nara's iconic 30,000 cherry trees), Naramachi park, Horyuji area sakura.
improvement_hint: Rank tourist-attraction spots (with tourism-oriented descriptions) above municipal event/administrative pages in get_spots output.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for remote Okinawa islands with stargazing. All returned results are from Ginowan City — a dense urban mainland Okinawa city. Utterly fails the 'remote island' constraint. Iriomote, Ishigaki, Miyako not represented.
missing_data: Iriomote, Ishigaki, Miyako-jima (actual dark-sky remote islands). Okinawa has multiple certified dark-sky areas on outlying islands.
improvement_hint: Add island/municipality sub-filter to enable routing queries to outlying island municipalities (Taketomi-cho, Miyakojima, etc.) rather than defaulting to large mainland cities.

reasoning: Query asks for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Tool returns hotels but mostly major city hotels (Sapporo ANA, Kushiro ANA). No onsen flag/filter. Some rural entries (Niseko area 'Full Circle', 'Gondola Chalet') exist but query intent for onsen-ryokan is unmet.
missing_data: Noboribetsu, Jozankei, Toyako, Sounkyo — Hokkaido's actual onsen resort towns with ryokan options.
improvement_hint: Add onsen/ryokan type tagging to hotel data so rural onsen properties can be filtered; currently hotel type='hotel' vs 'apartment' etc. doesn't distinguish onsen-ryokan.

reasoning: Kagawa query for Seto Inland Sea islands. Has useful content: Shodoshima stone history Japan Heritage, Tonosho-cho anime pilgrimage (Karakuri Jojo, 7 consecutive years), Kotohiki-yama hiking. But many generic admin pages dilute. Naoshima (art island) absent.
missing_data: Naoshima art island (Benesse House), Teshima, Ogijima; ferry connections from Takamatsu.
improvement_hint: Boost island-municipality pages (Tonosho-cho, Shodoshima-cho, Naoshima-cho) in ranking for Seto Inland Sea queries and add cross-prefecture linking to Hiroshima/Okayama islands.

reasoning: Returns 3 METI craft designations for Tokushima. Awa Shijira Weave (indigo fabric) and Awa Washi (indigo-dyed paper) are directly relevant. Otani-yaki (ceramics) is less so. Data is well-structured with English translations. However no workshop venue or experience listings.
missing_data: Actual workshop venues: Aizome Kobo in Tokushima, Awa Ai no Sato workshop center. Phone/hours/booking info absent.
improvement_hint: Link craft designations to workshop/experience venues in the municipal scrape data; craft designation alone doesn't let a traveler book an experience.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for Koya-san shukubo (temple lodging). Tool returns Wakayama hotels — generic business hotels (Hotel Kii Tanabe, Wakayama House, Nanki-Shirahama Marriott). No Koya-san temples or shukubo appear at all despite 50+ known shukubo on Koya-san.
missing_data: Eko-in, Koya-san Rengejo-in, Fudoin shukubo; Koya-san Okunoin. These are the canonical answer and completely absent.
improvement_hint: Add Koya-san municipality (Koya-cho) to the hotel/accommodation corpus; shukubo may need a dedicated accommodation type tag to surface in temple lodging queries.

reasoning: 89 matches for 武家屋敷. Best result: Kanazawa Nagamachi with rich description (Nomura-ke, Takada-ke, Ashigaru Museum, free volunteer guides). Also Saga, Ehime, Kyoto entries. Constraint 'outside Kyoto' not filtered. Kyoto result included. Mixed noise.
missing_data: Kakunodate (Akita) samurai district, Chiran (Kagoshima) samurai gardens — canonical off-beaten-path answers.
improvement_hint: Add a negative-filter option (e.g. exclude_prefecture) so 'outside Kyoto/Tokyo' queries can be enforced. Also improve ranking to surface named attraction pages over overview tourism pages.

reasoning: 39 Akita festivals in Indonesian. Includes real Akita festivals (Kakunodate, Tsuchizaki Shinmei). But also non-Akita items bleeding in (Hayachine Kagura from Iwate, Kawagoe Hikawa from Saitama) and no date filtering for 'summer'. Some items have null dates.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Festival (August, most famous summer festival), Noshiro Neburi Nagashi dates. Summer-specific filtering absent.
wrong_data: Hayachine Kagura is from Iwate Prefecture, not Akita — geographic data error in cross-prefecture bleeding.
improvement_hint: Fix geographic filtering to exclude non-Akita items; add season/month filtering for festivals so 'summer' returns only July-August items.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns in San'in. All results from Matsue City (prefectural capital): city hall opening, art exhibition, IT hackathon, cultural property repair notice. No fishing port towns. One entry mentions Mihonoseki briefly but it's buried.
missing_data: Mihonoseki (historic fishing/shipping port), Sakaiminato (fishing port/GeGeGe no Kitaro birthplace), Hamada, Ushimado. These are the answer.
improvement_hint: Improve geographic distribution within prefecture; currently Matsue city dominates Shimane results. Add coastal/port municipality coverage (Hamada, Sakaiminato, Mihonoseki).

reasoning: 9 Gifu craft items. Mino Washi (and likely Honmino Washi) directly relevant to the question. Hida Shunkei lacquerware also interesting. Data well-structured, English translated. But no experience/workshop info and 'Hidehira-nuri' is actually an Iwate craft incorrectly multi-coded to Gifu.
missing_data: Washi experience facilities: Udatsu no Machinami craft workshops in Mino City, specific studio booking info.
wrong_data: Hidehira-nuri (meti_densan:0502) lists Gifu (prefecture 21) as one code but its production area text is Morioka/Hanamaki/Ichinoseki (all Iwate). Cross-prefecture tag may be erroneous.
improvement_hint: Verify multi-prefecture craft assignments; Hidehira-nuri has no known Gifu production. Add workshop/experience venue links alongside craft designations.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages on Noto Peninsula. All results from Komatsu City (south Ishikawa, not Noto at all). Komatsu content includes kabuki festival, tea ceremony, event listings. Geographic mismatch: Komatsu is over 80km from the Noto Peninsula.
missing_data: Wajima (morning market, lacquerware), Sosogi, Noroshi, Suzu — Noto Peninsula's actual fishing communities.
improvement_hint: Improve geographic filtering; Ishikawa queries for 'Noto' should route to Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho, Anamizu municipalities, not default to Komatsu.

reasoning: 84 results for 砂丘. Top results are overwhelmingly Tottori (Tottori Dunes, Tottori Dunes rakkyo GI). Query explicitly asks for lesser-known dunes NOT Tottori. Constraint entirely unmet. One STAY YAMAGATA entry appears but not dune-related.
missing_data: Sakata Sand Dunes (Yamagata), Nakatajima Dunes (Shizuoka), Uchimido Dunes (Niigata), Enshuhama (Hamamatsu) — lesser-known Japan dunes.
improvement_hint: Support negative/exclusion constraints in search (e.g. 'not Tottori'); also improve result relevance so only sand-dune-type spots rank high for 砂丘 queries.

reasoning: 4 Shimane craft items in Chinese: Iwami Yaki (ceramics), Sekishu Washi (paper), Unshū Soroban (abacus), Izumo stone lanterns. All are genuine San'in crafts, correctly designated, well-translated. Covers ceramics, paper, woodcraft, stonework. Small count (4) but quality is high.
missing_data: Yasugi Doro-bune (Tottori side of San'in also deserves inclusion for full 山陰 coverage). Some craft descriptions lack workshop locations.
improvement_hint: Add Tottori prefecture to San'in craft queries (region-level queries should span both Shimane and Tottori); also include production town links for experience booking.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields beyond Furano. All returned spots are from Asahikawa and Iwamizawa: a library exhibition, an inbound tourism notice, a traveling art panel show, a children's center schedule. Zero lavender content. Furano itself absent too.

reasoning: Query in Filipino asks for traditional old towns in Kyushu. Tool returns Oita spots: Beppu WiFi info, Shidakako campsite, steam observation deck, wholesale market, children's crafts. Taketa City content (Christian heritage) is partially interesting but no 'traditional townscape' focus.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional machiya/kominka accommodation in Hokuriku. Returns generic Ishikawa hotels: unnamed student hostel, AB Hotel, ANA Crowne Plaza Kanazawa, Kanaya guesthouse. No kominka-specific properties. ANA Crowne Plaza is opposite of what's asked.

reasoning: 7 Japan Heritage items for Wakayama. First result 'Living with Whales' is off-topic for mountain shrines. Later items likely include Kumano Kodo pilgrimage route (relevant). Data well-structured, English translated at high confidence. But mountain shrine focus poorly served.
missing_data: Kumano Sanzan (Hongu, Hayatama, Nachi shrines) should surface prominently for Kii mountain shrine query. Yoshino-Omine (Nara side) also relevant.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals on the Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns intangible cultural heritage list: Dainichido Bugaku (Akita), Hayachine Kagura (Iwate), Kawagoe Hikawa (Saitama) — none are fireworks, none are Sea of Japan coast. Completely wrong data type.
missing_data: Nagaoka Fireworks (Niigata, Aug 2-3, largest on Sea of Japan), Ojiya Fireworks, Kashiwazaki City beach fireworks — the actual answer.

improvement_hint: Add fireworks/hanabi as a festival subtype in the data model; currently get_festivals only returns intangible heritage designations, missing the most commercially important summer events.

reasoning: 2 items for Oita crafts. Beppu Bamboo Crafts (別府竹細工) is exactly what was asked: METI designated, well-described (8 weaving techniques, Madake bamboo, production areas). Hakata Ori (weaving) is a bonus inclusion. Grounded, accurate, well-translated. Only weakness: no workshop links.
missing_data: Workshop venues: Beppu Traditional Bamboo Crafts Research Center (竹工芸訓練センター) which offers public viewing/classes.
improvement_hint: Link craft designations to associated experience venues or official craft centers; the Beppu bamboo research center is a key visitor attraction not captured here.

reasoning: Query asks for Shikoku cycling routes. Tool returns Ehime Wikidata entries: kofun (ancient burial mounds), local museums, Imabari Castle. The Shimanami Kaido (world-famous cycling route connecting Ehime to Hiroshima) is entirely absent. Wrong data type for route queries.

reasoning: 183 traditional arts items returned. Data includes kabuki as Important Intangible Cultural Property (Nogaku appears first, kabuki should be in list). However no venue or location data — just designations. Constraint 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' cannot be applied to undifferentiated national list.
